sections.title: What are fixed phrases?
sections.content: **Fixed phrases** (also called **set phrases** or **collocations**) are groups of words that are always used together in a particular order. You cannot replace one word with a synonym without changing the meaning or sounding wrong. For example: *in search of* (not *in search for*), *no longer* (not *not longer*), *in fact* (not *in reality* in that sense), *of course* (not *for course*).

sections.title: Types of fixed phrases
sections.content:
**Prepositional phrases:** *in search of, in spite of, in addition to, in charge of, in favour of, in terms of, on behalf of, on account of, as a result of, for the sake of.*
**Adverbial phrases:** *in fact, of course, at least, at first, at once, no longer, as well, as long as, as soon as, so far, once again.*
**Other set expressions:** *one of the, the first time, the same as, such as, as if, as though, would rather, had better.*

sections.title: How to learn them
sections.content: There is no single rule — you need to **learn each phrase as a chunk**. Reading and listening help, and so does practice. Tips: read the whole sentence and the ones around it — the phrase may span a clause (e.g. *not only … but also*). Learn phrases in context, not in isolation.

sections.title: Reference
sections.content: For a full list of fixed phrases, see the Fixed Phrases section under Reference.

questions.question: Which phrase is correct?
questions.explanation: The fixed phrase is **in search of** (e.g. in search of sunshine).

questions.question: Which phrase means 'not anymore'?
questions.explanation: **No longer** = not anymore (e.g. It is no longer available).

questions.question: Complete: _____ course, you can try again.
Complete: _____ long as you're careful, it's fine.
questions.explanation: **As long as** = on condition that; provided that.

questions.question: Which phrase is correct?
questions.explanation: The fixed phrase is **in search of** (e.g. in search of sunshine).

questions.question: Which phrase means 'not anymore'?
questions.explanation: **No longer** = not anymore (e.g. It is no longer available).

questions.question: Complete: _____ course, you can try again.
Complete: _____ long as you're careful, it's fine.
questions.explanation: **As long as** = on condition that; provided that.

questions.question: _____ fact, I've never been there. Which word?
This is one _____ the best films I've seen. Which word?
_____ doing so, they saved time. Which word?
questions.explanation: **By doing so** or **In doing so** = by/in doing that.

questions.question: In spite _____ the rain, we went out. Which word?
_____ addition to English, she speaks Spanish. Which word?
At _____, I didn't like him, but now I do. Which word?

gapfill_simple.title: Practice: Gap fill – Common fixed phrases

questions.question: They went abroad in search _____ sunshine.
It is no _____ available.
_____ fact, I've never been there.
_____ course, you're right.
_____ long as we're together, I'm happy.
This is one _____ the best films I've seen.
_____ doing so, they saved time.
questions.explanation: **By doing so** or **In doing so** = by/in doing that.

questions.question: In spite _____ the rain, we went out.
_____ addition to English, she speaks Spanish.
At _____, I didn't like him, but now I do.
_____ well as cooking, she cleans.
It was _____ first time I had flown.
_____ the end of the day, we were tired.
questions.explanation: **By the end of the day** (fixed phrase).

questions.question: The match was cancelled on account _____ the rain.
We went out in spite of the _____ that it was raining.
questions.explanation: **In spite of the fact that** = although.

questions.question: The _____ of the matter is that we need more time.
questions.explanation: **The truth of the matter** = the real fact; what is actually the case.

questions.question: Why don't you _____ a go? I'm sure you'll do well.
I've never tried sushi, but I'll _____ it a go.
questions.explanation: **Give it a go** = try it; attempt it (informal).

questions.question: She gave three reasons, _____ of which were convincing.
questions.explanation: **All of which** = referring to all the things just mentioned (in a relative clause).

questions.question: Prices rose to _____ an extent that many could not afford to buy.
questions.explanation: **To such an extent** = to so great a degree (often followed by *that*).
